spanish: Cuando vosotros decidís qué tipo de vídeos queréis consumir, hay que tener en cuenta dos tipos de habla.
english: When you decide what type of videos you want to consume, you have to keep in mind two types of speech.
explanation: Present subjunctive with 'cuando' referring to future - 'decidís' is present indicative but 'queréis' introduces a subordinate clause with implied intention

spanish: Si este es el único contenido que tú consumes, tienes que saber que estás entrenando tu oído para algo que no existe.
english: If this is the only content you consume, you have to know that you're training your ear for something that doesn't exist.
explanation: Conditional structure with 'si' (if) - present indicative in both clauses for real conditions, combined with relative clause 'que tú consumes'

spanish: Una persona que entiende estos programas perfectamente tiene un nivel C3.
english: A person who perfectly understands these programs has a C3 level.
explanation: Relative clause with indicative 'que entiende' - the indicative is used because the speaker presents this as a definite fact about capable people
